message: fix(wills-estate): copy-edit pass — handoff spelling, file count, consistent phrasing - "hand-off" → "handoff" (L856) to match rest of article - "two hundred files" → "over two hundred files" to reconcile with "201" stat chip - "beautifully-written" → "beautifully-drafted" (L1054) to match intro (L487) - "NOT" → "not" in Sub heading for semantic emphasis Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

  • @@ -1051,7 +1051,7 @@ jsm install wills-and-estate-planning-skill`}

    The most common failure mode in estate planning is not a drafting mistake. It is incoherence between documents. A - beautifully-written will that says + beautifully-drafted will that says “everything to my kids equally” does exactly nothing if the 401(k) still names the ex-spouse, because retirement accounts pass by contract, not by
